factors.summary: Demand is accelerating across all segments.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2023 increased by 15.8% as compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022. The components of revenue growth for the three months ended September 30, 2023 were as follows: Application Software Network Software Technology Enabled Products Roper Total Revenue Growth 24.8  % 5.0  % 10.1  % 15.8  %
evidence.subsection: Three months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand across all segments, supported by a 15.8% revenue increase.

evidence.text: Backlog increased 20% to $2,913.7 at September 30, 2023 as compared to $2,420.9 at September 30, 2022. Acquisitions contributed 16% and organic growth in backlog was 4%.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The backlog increased by 20%, indicating strong demand and growth.

factors.summary: Pricing power is evident through margin expansion.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased to 69.4% in the third quarter of 2023 as compared to 68.4% in the third quarter of 2022 due primarily to operating leverage on higher organic revenues combined with revenue mix.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross margin expansion suggests effective pricing strategies.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased to 69.4% in the third quarter of 2023 as compared to 68.4% in the third quarter of 2022 due primarily to operating leverage on higher organic revenues combined with revenue mix.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shift towards higher margins supports positive sentiment.

evidence.text: The growth of 4.5% in organic revenues was broad-based across the segment led by our businesses serving the property and casualty insurance, government contracting, and legal markets.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth acceleration across segments is a strong positive indicator.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased to 69.4% in the third quarter of 2023 as compared to 68.4% in the third quarter of 2022 due primarily to operating leverage on higher organic revenues combined with revenue mix.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expanding margins indicate operational efficiency and positive revenue dynamics.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering savings.

evidence.text: The dollar increase was due primarily to higher compensation expense.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher compensation expenses suggest cost pressures despite cost reduction efforts.

factors.summary: Management is confident with increased spending.

evidence.text: Corporate expenses increased to $62.3, or 4.0% of net revenues, in the third quarter of 2023 as compared to $55.2, or 4.1% of net revenues, in the third quarter of 2022. The dollar increase was due primarily to higher compensation expense.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Increased spending may indicate confidence but is offset by cost concerns.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include economic conditions and inflation.

evidence.text: These assumptions could prove inaccurate. Although we believe that the estimates and projections reflected in the forward-looking statements are reasonable, our expectations may prove to be incorrect. Important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from estimates or projections contained in the forward-looking statements include but are not limited to: • general economic conditions; • difficulty making acquisitions and successfully integrating acquired businesses; • any unforeseen liabilities associated with future acquisitions; • failure to effectively mitigate cybersecurity threats, including any litigation arising therefrom; • failure to comply with new data privacy laws and regulations, including any litigation arising therefrom; • risks and costs associated with our international sales and operations; • rising interest rates; • limitations on our business imposed by our indebtedness; • product liability, litigation, and insurance risks; • future competition; • reduction of business with large customers; • risks associated with government contracts; • changes in the supply of, or price for, labor, energy, raw materials, parts, and components, including as a result of impacts from the current inflationary environment, supply chain constraints, or additional or ongoing impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic; • potential write-offs of our goodwill and other intangible assets; • our ability to successfully develop new products; • failure to protect our intellectual property; • unfavorable changes in foreign exchange rates; • difficulties associated with exports/imports and risks of changes to tariff rates; • increased warranty exposure; • environmental compliance costs and liabilities; • the effect of, or change in, government regulations (including tax); • risks associated with the use of artificial intelligence; • economic disruption caused by armed conflicts (such as the war in Ukraine and the conflict in the Middle East), terrorist attacks, health crises (such as the COVID-19 pandemic), or other unforeseen geopolitical events; and • the factors discussed in other reports we file with the SEC from time to time.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The evidence lists multiple significant risks including economic conditions, inflation, and other disruptions, indicating moderate headwinds.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is clear with focus on acquisitions and R&D.

evidence.text: We pursue consistent and sustainable growth in revenue, earnings, and cash flow by enabling continuous improvement in the operating performance of our businesses and by acquiring other businesses that offer high value-added software, services, technology-enabled products, and solutions that we believe are capable of achieving growth and maintaining high margins.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear long-term strategy focused on acquisitions and R&D, which are positive for future growth.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include interest rates and economic conditions.

evidence.text: Important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from estimates or projections contained in the forward-looking statements include but are not limited to: • general economic conditions; • difficulty making acquisitions and successfully integrating acquired businesses; • any unforeseen liabilities associated with future acquisitions; • failure to effectively mitigate cybersecurity threats, including any litigation arising therefrom; • failure to comply with new data privacy laws and regulations, including any litigation arising therefrom; • risks and costs associated with our international sales and operations; • rising interest rates; • limitations on our business imposed by our indebtedness; • product liability, litigation, and insurance risks; • future competition; • reduction of business with large customers; • risks associated with government contracts; • changes in the supply of, or price for, labor, energy, raw materials, parts, and components, including as a result of impacts from the current inflationary environment, supply chain constraints, or additional or ongoing impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic; • potential write-offs of our goodwill and other intangible assets; • our ability to successfully develop new products; • failure to protect our intellectual property; • unfavorable changes in foreign exchange rates; • difficulties associated with exports/imports and risks of changes to tariff rates; • increased warranty exposure; • environmental compliance costs and liabilities; • the effect of, or change in, government regulations (including tax); • risks associated with the use of artificial intelligence; • economic disruption caused by armed conflicts (such as the war in Ukraine and the conflict in the Middle East), terrorist attacks, health crises (such as the COVID-19 pandemic), or other unforeseen geopolitical events; and • the factors discussed in other reports we file with the SEC from time to time.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is sensitive to factors like rising interest rates and economic conditions, which could negatively impact performance.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new product launches and successful integrations.

evidence.text: In our Application Software segment, revenues were $803.4 in the third quarter of 2023 as compared to $644.0 in the third quarter of 2022. The growth of 4.5% in organic revenues was broad-based across the segment led by our businesses serving the property and casualty insurance, government contracting, and legal markets.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New product launches and successful integrations are catalysts for growth, indicating a positive outlook.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures were $37.8 for the nine months ended September 30, 2023 as compared to $30.0 for the nine months ended September 30, 2022. Capitalized software expenditures were $28.7 for the nine months ended September 30, 2023 as compared to $21.9 for the nine months ended September 30, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures signal confidence in growth opportunities and expansion plans.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable or slightly increasing.

evidence.text: Total debt consisted of the following: As of September 30, 2023 Fixed-rate senior notes $ 6,000.0  Unsecured credit facility 910.0  Deferred financing costs (31.9) Other 0.2  Total debt, net of deferred financing costs 6,878.3  Less: Current portion 499.3  Long-term debt, net of deferred financing costs $ 6,379.0
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable or slightly increasing debt leverage is a mixed signal, neither clearly positive nor negative.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities increased by 157% to $1,415.7 in the nine months ended September 30, 2023 as compared to $550.2 in the nine months ended September 30, 2022 primarily due to (i) cash taxes paid in the prior period in connection with the 2021 Divestitures, (ii) higher net earnings from continuing operations net of non-cash expenses, and (iii) lower cash taxes paid primarily due to tax benefits associated with the Frontline acquisition.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significantly improving free cash flow indicates strong financial health and positive developments.

factors.summary: MA activity is creating value through successful acquisitions.

evidence.text: Cash used in investing activities from continuing operations during the nine months ended September 30, 2023 was primarily for business acquisitions, most notably Syntellis and Replicon.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active and successful mergers and acquisitions indicate strategic growth and value creation.

evidence.text: We expect existing cash balances, together with cash generated by our operations and amounts available under our credit facility, will be sufficient to fund our operating requirements for the foreseeable future.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates favorable economic conditions, supported by sufficient cash and credit facility access.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are having a mixed impact.

evidence.text: Interest expense, net, increased to $42.4 for the third quarter of 2023 as compared to $41.3 for the third quarter of 2022 due to borrowings on our unsecured credit facility, partially offset by lower weighted average fixed-rate debt balances and higher interest income earned on our cash and cash equivalents.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows a mixed impact with increased interest expense offset by lower debt and higher interest income.
